title: 10 best herbal teas for cystitis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for cystitis are a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections (UTIs) and reduce inflammation. These teas work by soothing the bladder lining, reducing bacteria growth, and improving urine flow. Examples include corn silk tea, which helps to coat the bladder and soothe irritation, and uva ursi tea, which has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can experience relief from symptoms such as burning urination, frequent trips to the bathroom, and discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry teas helps with cystitis because its primary active compound, arbutin, has potent antibacterial properties that target the E. coli bacteria responsible for most bladder infections. The tea's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects also soothe the bladder lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, bearberry's natural astringent properties help to shrink swollen tissues and reduce urine production, alleviating symptoms such as burning sensations while urinating. This holistic approach can provide effective relief from cystitis symptoms without relying on antibiotics or other harsh medications.
remedy_properties: Uricosuric activity: Bearberry tea has been shown to increase the excretion of urates from the body, which can help reduce the concentration of uric acid in the urine and alleviate symptoms of cystitis.

Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial compounds present in bearberry tea, such as arbutin, have been found to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause urinary tract infections (UTIs), including Escherichia coli (E. coli) and Staphylococcus saprophyticus.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Bearberry tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the bladder and urinary tract associated with cystitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bearberry plant are used to make medicinal teas as they contain high concentrations of antimicrobial compounds that can help combat bacterial infections causing cystitis.
Barks: Bearberry barks have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections, including cystitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried leaves of arctostaphylos uva ursi in an amount equaling one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Boil filtered or bottled water for five minutes in a teapot or kettle.
Add the dried leaves to the boiling water and let steep for ten to fifteen minutes to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove any solid particles.
Consume the tea immediately while still warm, repeating the process up to three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with cystitis because it provides a soothing and protective barrier for the urinary tract. The mucilages in marshmallow root coat and calm irritated bladder tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow tea help to alleviate symptoms such as burning and frequency. By providing a gentle and non-irritating environment, marshmallow teas can promote healing and reduce the risk of recurring cystitis episodes.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains a thickening agent called mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herb contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of cystitis such as burning sensation while urinating.

Astringent properties: Marshmallow tea's astringent properties can help constrict the urinary tract and reduce the frequency and urgency of urination, providing relief from the discomfort associated with cystitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of althaea officinalis plant and dry them thoroughly using a food dehydrator or air circulation method for 24 hours.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried althaea officinalis leaves per cup of boiling water and steep the mixture for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a heat-resistant cup to remove solids and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature before consuming it within 30 minutes of preparation.
Drink two to three cups of althaea officinalis tea per day, ideally with meals, for a minimum duration of one week to alleviate cystitis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn teas helps with cystitis because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the urinary tract and reduce inflammation. The herbal ingredients in corn tea, such as marshmallow root and uva ursi, have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of cystitis, including painful urination and frequent trips to the bathroom. By calming the bladder and reducing discomfort, corn teas provide natural relief from cystitis without the need for pharmaceutical medications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Antioxidants in corn tea, such as polyphenols, help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, which can contribute to the development of cystitis.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids found in corn tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis, such as burning sensation while urinating, frequency, and urgency.

Antimicrobial activity: The antimicrobial properties of corn tea, particularly the presence of beta-carotene and other carotenoids, may help combat bacterial infections in the urinary tract that can contribute to cystitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to inhibit bacterial growth and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh corn silk and rinse them thoroughly under cool running water.
Combine the rinsed corn silk with 4 cups of boiling water in a large pot and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove any solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root and 1 teaspoon of dried uva ursi leaves to the tea and simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Allow the tea to cool before straining it again and storing it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod teas helps with cystitis because its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and discomfort in the urinary tract. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in goldenrod tea have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause urinary tract infections, including E. coli. Additionally, goldenseal, a common ingredient in herbal blends, contains berberine, which has been proven to combat bacterial adhesion to bladder walls, further alleviating symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Goldenrod tea has been shown to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis such as burning and frequency.
Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal compounds present in goldenrod tea can help combat infections that cause cystitis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Uric acid-reducing properties: Goldenrod has been traditionally used to reduce uric acid levels in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis caused by excess uric acid crystals in the urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenrod plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with cystitis.
Buds: Goldenrod buds have been traditionally used to make a tea that helps soothe bladder irritation and reduce symptoms of cystitis due to their high content of flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried solidago virgaurea leaves and flowers from a trusted source.
Combine the harvested herbs with 2 cups of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reached a comfortable drinking temperature.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean teapot or mug to remove any solids.
Serve the tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and consume within an hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zucchini teas helps with cystitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the bladder and reduce discomfort. The antioxidants present in zucchini tea also help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation, promoting a healthy urinary tract. Additionally, the diuretic properties of zucchini tea increase urine production, helping to flush out bacteria and reduce the risk of infection. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to manage cystitis symptoms without harsh medication or treatment.
remedy_properties: Uva Ursi (Bearberry): The leaf of this herb contains arbutin, a compound that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the urinary tract.

Marshmallow: Marshmallow root has been used for centuries to soothe bladder irritations and reduce inflammation. Its mucilages (thick, gel-like substances) can help protect the bladder lining from further irritation and reduce pain during urination.
Stinging Nettle: Stinging nettle leaf has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of cystitis, such as painful urination.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the zucchini plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated bladder tissues, reducing symptoms of cystitis.
Seeds: Zucchini seeds have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections, including cystitis, due to their antiseptic and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried cucurbita pepo flowers and leaves in amounts of one teaspoon per eight ounces.
Boil filtered water in a saucepan for five minutes before preparing the tea.
Add the dried herbs to the boiling water and allow it to steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature of 80 to 90 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with cystitis because of its unique combination of diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids work together to soothe the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with the condition. Additionally, dandelion's natural diuretic properties help increase urine production, flushing out bacteria and other toxins that can contribute to cystitis. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from symptoms of cystitis, offering a gentle and effective alternative to pharmaceutical treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which may help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of cystitis such as burning and discomfort.

Antibacterial properties: Dandelion tea has antibacterial properties that can help combat bacterial infections, such as Escherichia coli (E. coli), which are common causes of cystitis, and prevent the spread of infection in the urinary tract.

Diuretic effects: Dandelion tea is a natural diuretic, meaning it can increase urine production, which may help flush out bacteria and other toxins that contribute to cystitis, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe urinary tract inflammation associated with cystitis.
Rhyzomes: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective against bacterial infections and reducing the severity of cystitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried taraxacum officinale herb into a clean glass teapot or infuser.
Heat 200ml of filtered water in a kettle or microwave for 30-40 seconds to 90°c.
Pour the hot water over the taraxacum officinale herb and allow it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, squeezing the solids gently to release all the liquid.
Serve the tea immediately, adding honey or lemon if desired, and consume within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry teas helps with cystitis because its unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and berberine alkaloids offers potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Berbarine has been shown to inhibit bacterial growth, reducing the severity and frequency of urinary tract infections that often cause cystitis symptoms such as burning urination and abdominal pain. Additionally, the tea's soothing and astringent properties help to calm irritated bladder tissues, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
remedy_properties: Berberine: A bioactive alkaloid that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce bacterial growth and alleviate inflammation in the urinary tract.

Volatile oils: Compounds like limonene and terpinen-4-ol have antiseptic and analgesic properties, which help soothe pain and discomfort associated with cystitis, as well as reduce the risk of infection.
Anthraquinone glycosides: These compounds have a laxative effect, helping to flush out bacteria and toxins from the urinary tract and prevent recurrent infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used in teas to treat cystitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
Barks: Barberry barks contain berberine, a compound with antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and prevent infections in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried berberis vulgaris leaves from the local market or store them in an air-tight container for future use.
Boil one cup of filtered water in a pot and pour it over the harvested berberis vulgaris leaves for three to five minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep for ten to fifteen minutes, then strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink two cups of the cooled berberis vulgaris tea three times a day, starting from the onset of symptoms and continuing until improvement is observed.
Store any remaining tea in an air-tight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before re-steeping or disposing of it properly.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Buchu tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as quercetin and kaempferol, help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of cystitis like burning and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: Buchu tea's antimicrobial compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause cystitis, such as Escherichia coli (E. coli), reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
Urinary tract relaxant properties: Buchu tea's active ingredients, like ursolic acid, help to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can worsen cystitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help alleviate symptoms of cystitis by reducing inflammation and infection.
Rhyzomes: Contain high levels of volatile oils that provide antiseptic properties, making them effective against bacterial infections that cause cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 teaspoons of dried agathosma betulina leaves and flowers or 3-4 grams of fresh material.
Measure 250ml of boiling water and steep the herb mixture for 5-7 minutes in a teapot.
Strain the tea by pouring it through a filter or cheesecloth to remove solids and discard the herbs.
Add honey to taste and stir well before serving the tea hot or warm after cooling down slightly.
Consume the tea immediately and repeat as needed up to three times daily for best results.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Buchu teas helps with cystitis because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The tea's antioxidant-rich compounds also help to eliminate bacteria and other irritants from the bladder, promoting a healthy balance of flora and fauna in the urinary system. Additionally, buchu tea's diuretic properties help to flush out toxins and excess fluids, providing relief from painful urination and frequency.

Ginger teas helps with cystitis because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the discomfort and pain associated with this condition. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to combat inflammation in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating symptoms like burning sensation while urinating and frequent urination. Additionally, ginger's antibacterial properties may also help to prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate cystitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and alleviate the discomfort associated with cystitis.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents in ginger tea, including citral and geraniol, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause urinary tract infections (UTIs), a common underlying cause of cystitis.
Spasmolytic properties: Ginger's spasmolytic compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing spasms and pain associated with cystitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties help reduce pain, discomfort, and frequency of urination associated with cystitis.
Buds: Ginger buds contain antioxidants and have natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the bladder, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots and peel them to remove excess skin.
Chop the peeled ginger into small pieces and measure out one teaspoon per cup.
Heat filtered water in a kettle or pot to a rolling boil for 5-7 minutes.
Add the chopped ginger to the boiling water and steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry teas helps with cystitis because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause urinary tract infections, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis. Additionally, highbush cranberry's diuretic properties help flush out toxins and reduce symptoms like burning sensation while urinating. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to alleviate cystitis symptoms without resorting to harsh medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Highbush cranberry tea has been shown to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis such as pain and burning during urination.
Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial compounds have been found to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause cystitis, helping to prevent infections from occurring or recurring.

Urinary antiseptic properties: Highbush cranberry tea has been traditionally used as a natural treatment for urinary tract infections due to its ability to cleanse and disinfect the urinary system, helping to eliminate bacterial toxins and reduce symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing bladder discomfort.
Fruits: Rich in vitamins, minerals, and anthocyanins, which help to reduce UTI symptoms by inhibiting bacterial adhesion and preventing biofilm formation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one ounce of dried vaccinium macrocarpon leaves or berries for every two cups of boiling water needed.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried herb for every cup of boiling water and pour it into a teapot.
Pour boiling water over the herbal mixture and let it steep for 5-10 minutes to allow flavors to meld together.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove any remaining solids and enjoy hot or chilled as desired.
Drink two to three cups of the tea throughout the day, ideally at 30-minute intervals, for optimal relief from cystitis symptoms.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cystitis with herbal teas is to drink a combination of teas that have natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. A blend of uva ursi, marshmallow root, and chamomile tea can help reduce symptoms such as burning and urgency. Uva ursi specifically has been shown to inhibit bacterial growth and relieve urinary discomfort. Drink at least three cups a day for optimal relief.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cystitis that are treated with herbal teas are urinary tract infections, bladder stones, and urethritis. Uva ursi tea has been traditionally used to treat UTIs due to its antimicrobial properties. Dandelion root tea helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of bladder stones, while nettle leaf tea soothes the bladder and urinary tract, providing relief from urethritis. These herbal teas may also help prevent future occurrences of these conditions.
